commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v31.7: clip ext glows to actual image bounds (fix object-fit:contain margins)

Previous clipPath used full 0,0,100,100 SVG coords, but ext-img uses
object-fit:contain so black pillarbox/letterbox margins exist where the
image doesn't reach. Added updateExtVpClip() which calculates the real
displayed image rect within the container and updates the #ext-vp-clip rect
to match — called on img load, applyViewMode, and sizeAndAlign so it stays
accurate on resize and split-view toggle.

commit.message:
Refactor: split monolith into internal packages

Split the 2092-line main.go and 2140-line main_test.go into well-bounded
internal packages for readability and testability:

- internal/envutil: file discovery, shell quoting, validation patterns
- internal/template: {{SECRET}} template rendering
- internal/merge: 3-way merge algorithms (pure, no internal deps)
- internal/crypto: AES-GCM encryption, age key wrapping, HMAC-SHA256 MAC
- internal/keyring: OS keyring access, key derivation
- internal/sshkeys: SSH-to-age conversion, ed25519 key discovery
- internal/github: GitHub CLI integration, SSH key fetching
- internal/encfile: encrypted file I/O, encrypt/decrypt, auto-resolve
- internal/cli: all cobra commands, styles, Execute()
- internal/testutil: shared test helpers

Key design change: decryptSecrets and reWrapDataKey now accept privateKey
as a parameter instead of calling getKey() internally, eliminating the
encfile → keyring dependency and making these functions trivially testable.

Tests converted to table-driven where appropriate (merge, template, MAC
tampering). main.go reduced to 6 lines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v31.6: fix admin buttons clipped by switch-picker overflow:hidden

When admin mode is toggled (sun/moon click), toggle .admin-open on
#switch-picker so it switches to height:auto;overflow:visible, allowing
the wrapped button rows to fully show instead of being cut off at 50px.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: Run auth init in background to prevent 1-min page load delay

Previously, DOMContentLoaded awaited AuthModule.init() which calls
supabase.auth.getSession() - a network request. Users with cached
sessions had to wait ~1 minute for Supabase to respond before
the home screen rendered.

Auth now runs in background (non-blocking). Router.init() and
HomeScreen render immediately. Auth UI updates asynchronously
once session is confirmed.

- Replace all CJK 析 (U+6790) → Korean 석 (U+C11D) in 23 source files

commit.message: Fix CORS image blocking and reveal animation MovieCard: remove crossOrigin='anonymous' from canvas code — TMDB CDN doesn't return CORS headers, so this was poisoning Chrome's cache and blocking all poster images from loading in tags. reveal: replace JS IntersectionObserver approach (opacity:0 + is-visible) with a pure CSS animation that fires automatically on render, eliminating the race condition where the observer ran before async content loaded. Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6

style: rename unused loop variables to suppress ruff B007

repository.name: wavekit
repository.description: a fundamental Python package for digital circuit waveform analysis

commit.message:
Optimize chart rendering — LTTB decimation, visibility gating, efficient trimming

- Add Chart.js Decimation plugin with LTTB algorithm (500 samples) for ranges > 5min
- Use IntersectionObserver to skip chart updates for off-screen cards
- Replace per-element shift() loops with single splice() for data trimming
- Switch from Date objects to numeric ms timestamps (required for parsing: false)
- Set pointRadius: 0 on single-metric charts to eliminate thousands of arc draws
- Use tension: 0.4 for short ranges (smooth curves), tension: 0 for long ranges (enables decimation)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v31.5: clip ext light glows via SVG clipPath (not just CSS overflow)

CSS overflow:hidden doesn't stop SVG filter effects from bleeding outside
the viewport. Added a static <clipPath id="ext-vp-clip"> (full 0,0,100,100
viewport rect) to ext-svg, and apply clip-path="url(#ext-vp-clip)" to every
lightG so deck and side entry cone glows are hard-clipped to the frame.

commit.message:
Improve prediction popup latency by caching domain settings and removing tabs.get pre-flight (#291)

Two performance bottlenecks were causing ~15-25 ms of avoidable overhead on
every keystroke prediction:

1. `resolveDomainRuntimeSettings` was called on every prediction request,
   performing 5 parallel chrome.storage reads (~5-15 ms) even though domain
   settings virtually never change between keystrokes.
   Fix: introduce `DomainSettingsCache` (500 ms TTL, per-domain key) in
   `MessageRouter`.  The cache is immediately invalidated when
   `CMD_OPTIONS_PAGE_CONFIG_CHANGE` is received so settings changes still
   take effect without waiting for expiry.

2. `BackgroundServiceWorker.runPrediction` called `chrome.tabs.get` before
   every `sendMessage` as a tab-existence pre-flight, adding ~5-10 ms of
   extra IPC latency.  `sendMessage` already throws when the tab/frame is
   gone, so the pre-flight was redundant.
   Fix: call `chrome.tabs.sendMessage` directly and catch the error.

Performance gain per keystroke: ~15-25 ms less background-side overhead.
50 consecutive predictions for the same domain now incur 1 storage read
instead of 250.

Tests added:
- `tests/DomainSettingsCache.test.ts` — 11 unit tests covering cache hit/miss,
  TTL expiry, per-domain isolation, invalidation, and a latency benchmark
  asserting cached requests are ≥5× faster than uncached ones.
- Two regression tests in `tests/background.routing.test.ts`:
  - Verifies `chrome.tabs.get` is NOT called during prediction dispatch.
  - Verifies the domain settings cache is invalidated after a config change
    so the next prediction sees fresh language/settings values.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs: comprehensive documentation overhaul and test scaffolding

- README.md: full rewrite with badges, architecture diagram, tech stack,
  8-step quick start (incl. CREATE DATABASE superset), env var reference,
  dbt/Kafka/Schema Registry run instructions, Helm section, design decisions,
  contribution guide, and links to all new docs
- SYSTEM_DESIGN.md: rewrite covering data flow diagrams (ingestion →
  transformation → serving), component responsibility table, SLAs/SLOs,
  failure mode matrix with cascading scenarios and SPOFs, scalability levers
  per component, exactly-once semantics analysis, and cost model
- DATA_DICTIONARY.md: column-level docs for Kafka Avro schema,
  raw_retail_sales, agg_store_sales_5min, fact_sales; lineage diagram;
  env var reference table
- RUNBOOK.md: on-call guide for 5 failure scenarios (Spark crash, Airflow
  short-circuit, Kafka producer failure, dbt failure, data quality alert)
- REPO_HEALTH.md: full audit — architecture (A1-A10), DAG (D1-D6), dbt
  (M1-M5), test coverage gaps, data quality (Q1-Q5), CI/CD, monitoring
  (OB1-OB6), security (S1-S7), PII (P1-P4), deps (V1-V5), cost (C1-C5),
  env parity (E1-E4); master prioritised backlog
- .github/workflows/ci.yml: 7-job GitHub Actions CI — TruffleHog secret
  scan, flake8, sqlfluff, dbt compile (mock profile), pytest, Docker build
  validation, pip-audit
- tests/test_producer.py: 11 unit tests for Avro schema + message generation
- tests/test_stream_processor.py: 12 unit tests for Kafka/Snowflake config
  and Avro schema parsing (no live Spark/Snowflake required)

commit.message:
fix: remove dangerous fallback UUID from createConversation

The catch block was generating a random UUID as fallback when the API
call failed. This made the chat appear to work but all subsequent
messages would 404 since the ID didn't exist in the database. Now the
error propagates to useChat which shows a proper error message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Modernize build system and security

Security improvements:
- Add helmet middleware for security headers
- Add HSTS header to nginx config
- Update Express 4.13 to 4.21
- Update jQuery 2.1 to 3.7 (security patches)

Build system modernization:
- Migrate Gulp 3 to Gulp 4 (rewrite gulpfile.js)
- Update Babel 5 to Babel 7 with @babel/preset-env
- Replace gulp-uglify with gulp-terser for modern JS
- Remove deprecated gulp-browserify
- Update vinyl-source-stream and vinyl-buffer

Dependency updates:
- body-parser, cookie-parser, morgan, compression
- babelify 6 to 10
- del 5 for Gulp 4 compatibility

Vulnerabilities reduced from 97 to 56 (42% reduction)

[DOCS] Documentar ruta fiable para ficheros temporales en Windows

docs_prueba/temp/ (gitignored) como ruta canónica.
